title: Finding taxable income
problem: James earns a gross income of $\$72\,000$ per year and claims allowable deductions of $\$3\,500$. Find his taxable income.

steps.instruction: Write the formula for taxable income.
Subtract to find the taxable income.

title: Medicare levy and annual PAYG tax withheld
problem: Aisha's annual taxable income is $\$69\,000$. The Medicare levy is $2\%$ of taxable income. A fortnightly tax table shows that $\$489$ is withheld each fortnight in PAYG tax. Calculate (i) the Medicare levy and (ii) the total PAYG tax withheld over the year.

steps.instruction: Calculate the Medicare levy using the rate of 2%.
Identify the number of fortnights in a year.
Multiply the fortnightly PAYG amount by 26 to find the annual total.

title: Net income and tax refund from a tax return
problem:
Raj's taxable income is $\$65\,000$. His employer withheld $\$14\,000$ in PAYG tax during the year. Using the tax table below and a Medicare levy of $2\%$, determine whether Raj receives a refund or owes a payment, and state the amount.

| Taxable income | Tax on this income |
|---|---|
| $\$45\,001$ – $\$120\,000$ | $\$5\,092$ plus $32.5$ cents for each $\$1$ over $\$45\,000$ |

steps.instruction: Identify the tax bracket and calculate income tax.
Find total tax liability by adding income tax and Medicare levy.
Compare total tax with PAYG withheld to determine refund or payment.
